title: Calculate a transparent linear pace state
description: Compare month-to-date recorded actuals with an exact rational Linear Pace Baseline while exposing its uniform-spend limitation. Goal trace: G2.
rationale: A simple reproducible baseline can answer whether recorded spend is above or below a linear share without pretending to predict future behavior.
story_want: compare recorded month-to-date spend with one transparent plan-relative baseline
story_so_that: pace is reproducible and never mistaken for a Forecast
acceptance_criteria: Given a started Budget Period with a plan, when a Linear Pace Baseline is calculated, then elapsed calendar days include the As-of local date and total calendar days equal the complete Budget Period length.

Given a non-zero planned amount, when the baseline is calculated, then it equals planned minor units multiplied by elapsed days divided by total days as an exact rational value with no floating-point arithmetic.

Given exact recorded actual and the exact rational baseline, when pace is classified, then actual greater than the full planned amount returns Over Plan; otherwise exact comparison with the baseline returns Below Linear Baseline, At Linear Baseline, or Above Linear Baseline.
Given a zero planned amount with positive recorded actual, when pace is classified, then Over Zero Plan is returned without division.
Given a zero planned amount with zero or negative recorded actual, when pace is classified, then At Or Below Zero Plan is returned without division.

Given no plan, no active or explicit revision, a not-started period, or unavailable coherent actual evidence, when pace is requested, then the exact named unavailable state is returned and no baseline is fabricated.

Given any Linear Pace result, when its explanation is returned, then it states the elapsed-day fraction, formula version, exact inputs, current-day incompleteness possibility, and that the baseline assumes uniform calendar distribution and is not a Forecast.
Given daily or weekly exact metrics, when pace output is inspected, then no separate daily or weekly sub-budget is invented from the monthly plan.
